title: Aggregate with FILTER clause via fragment
prompt: Write `MyApp.Blog.per_category_published_count/0` that returns each category with two counts: `total_posts` (all posts) AND `published_posts` (only published). Use PostgreSQL FILTER: `fragment("COUNT(?) FILTER (WHERE ?)", p.id, p.published)` for the filtered count. Group by category. Write to `lib/my_app/blog.ex`.
tier_rationale: FILTER clause has no typed Ecto equivalent — must use fragment. Getting BOTH counts in one query avoids a second round-trip.
